plan.action: Verify lib/claude.sh already implements claude_is_installed() (uses 'command -v claude') and claude_validate() (prints actionable ERROR/Install/PATH messages and returns 1 when missing). Both functions are present and complete.
Verify clippy.sh already calls claude_validate() in main() before any loop work (line 141-143) and exits 1 on failure — satisfying the 'loop does not begin' criterion.
Verify tests/claude.bats already covers claude_is_installed (present/absent) and claude_validate (returns 0, returns 1, prints ERROR, prints Install hint, mentions PATH). All 5 unit tests exist.
Add a missing integration test to tests/cli.bats: 'clippy exits non-zero when claude binary is absent from PATH' — verifying the end-to-end loop-does-not-begin behaviour with an empty PATH stub dir.
Run 'bats tests/claude.bats tests/cli.bats' to confirm all tests pass and the acceptance criteria are fully met.

testing_recommendations: Unit test claude_is_installed returns 0 when a 'claude' stub exists in PATH
Unit test claude_is_installed returns non-zero when PATH contains no 'claude' binary
Unit test claude_validate returns 0 when claude binary is present
Unit test claude_validate returns 1 when claude binary is absent
Unit test claude_validate stderr contains 'ERROR' when claude is missing
Unit test claude_validate stderr contains install instruction ('Install') when claude is missing
Unit test claude_validate stderr mentions 'PATH' when claude is missing
Integration test (cli.bats): clippy.sh exits non-zero when claude is absent from PATH before any loop work begins
Integration test (cli.bats): clippy.sh prints an actionable error message when claude is absent
risks: lib/claude.sh and clippy.sh already implement all required behaviour — the primary risk is that the PRD prd.json still has passes:false because the integration test in cli.bats covering 'no claude → exit 1' has not been written yet; adding that test should make the story pass
shellcheck must not flag the new test or existing implementation — use 'command -v' (already correct) rather than 'which' to remain POSIX-safe
